prompt: A 17th-century scientific engraving depicting gradient descent as a physical process. A detailed mountainous landscape represents a cost function, rendered with fine etched lines and cross-hatching. A small sphere descends step-by-step down the slope toward a valley minimum, with multiple positions shown along the path. Arrows indicate direction of movement. Annotated with Latin-style handwritten labels such as "Iteratio I, II, III", "Gradus", and "Minima". The composition follows early modern scientific diagrams from the Age of Exploration, with monochrome ink, precise linework, and aged parchment texture. No modern elements, no digital shading, no color.
concept: Gradient Descent optimization in machine learning
tradition_name: Scientific engraving from the Age of Exploration
tradition_approach: Used cross-hatching, monochrome ink, Latin annotations, and diagrammatic composition to match early scientific engravings explaining natural phenomena.
